mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate, increasing the present value of expected corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, it signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations down over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence recovery, generating strong positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a bivariate regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion phases, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, triggering short-term momentum buying in equities.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns specifically during early-cycle regimes, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 50% in those periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, which systematically supports higher equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no significant positive relationship with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly CPI inflation accelerates beyond 0.5%, it triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL monthly changes exceed 0.5%, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with interaction term coefficients insignificant in multivariate models over a 2-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle peaks, declining housing starts signal weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced construction activity, forecasting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Declining HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns specifically in late-cycle regimes, with directional accuracy below 45% in those periods over a 3-year rolling evaluation.
